title_en: Marine Le Pen is running for the French presidency
title_zh: 玛丽娜·勒庞竞选法国总统
rubric_en: Despite being found guilty by the Paris court of appeal
rubric_zh: 尽管巴黎上诉法院认定她有罪

paragraphs.en: FOR OVER half a century, a member of the Le Pen family has stood at every French presidential election bar one. Then, after a court in 2025 banned Marine Le Pen, the leader of the populist-right National Rally (RN), from running for elected office for five years, it had looked as if the presidential vote next year would mark the end of a dynastic era.
paragraphs.zh: 在过去半个多世纪里，除了一次之外，勒庞家族的成员都曾参与过每一次法国总统选举。随后，2025年一家法庭以勒庞领衔的民粹右派“国民联盟”（RN）候选人不得参选公职五年，禁止其参选，使人们一度认为明年总统投票将宣告这个家族时代的终结。

paragraphs.en: But on July 7th, in an unexpected twist, the court of appeal in Paris upheld Ms Le Pen’s conviction for the misuse of European Parliament funds for party ends—yet lightened her sentence, freeing her to run. Hours later Ms Le Pen announced that she, not Jordan Bardella, her 30-year-old lieutenant, would make a bid for the presidency after all.
paragraphs.zh: 但在7月7日，巴黎上诉法院出人意料地做出裁决，维持了勒庞女士因将欧洲议会议员资金挪作党务用途的定罪，却缓和了其刑期，从而使她得以参选。几小时后，勒庞女士宣布，最终她而不是她的30岁副手乔丹·巴德拉将会角逐总统。

paragraphs.en: The RN leader’s decision ends 15 months of uncertainty, and sets France up for a highly unusual election. In its ruling, the court of appeal handed her a three-year jail sentence; two are suspended, and one is to be served with an electronic ankle tag. Crucially for Ms Le Pen, the court also shortened her ban and ruled that she had already served the ineligibility penalty.
paragraphs.zh: 这位“国民联盟”领导人的决定结束了15个月的不确定性，也让法国走向一场极不寻常的选举。在裁决中，上诉法院给她判处三年监禁；其中两年缓刑，一年须佩戴电子脚镣执行。对勒庞女士而言，最关键的是，法院还缩短了她的参选禁令期限，并裁定她已经执行完了这一资格剥夺处罚。

paragraphs.en: In announcing her candidacy, Ms Le Pen said that the ban had posed “an enormous democratic problem” and that its ending changed everything, enabling “the French people to judge”. That ruling, in effect, took the political decision out of the judges’ hands and put it firmly into hers. Ms Le Pen continues to claim that she is innocent. She is now taking her case to the highest court, the Cour de Cassation, in part to contest the requirement that she wear a tag (which will not be imposed while the appeal is under way). That court says it could review her case before the election next April and May. If it rejects her appeal, she will have to campaign while wearing the tag.
paragraphs.zh: 在宣布参选时，勒庞女士表示，禁令造成了“一个巨大的民主难题”，而其解除后改变了一切，使“法国人民能够来裁决”。这一裁决实际上把政治判断从法官手中拿走，牢牢交到她自己手里。勒庞女士仍坚持自己是清白的。她现在将案件提交到最高法院——“最高法院”（Cour de Cassation）的一部分程序，是为了质疑必须佩戴电子脚镣的要求（该要求在上诉期间不会执行）。该法院表示，可能在明年四月和五月的选举前审理此案。如果她的上诉被驳回，她将不得不在佩戴脚镣的情况下继续竞选活动。

paragraphs.en: For the RN, her decision brings clarity and will allow the party to begin its campaign proper. Ms Le Pen will have the authority to shape its direction, take decisions on which there is policy disagreement (she wants to reduce the legal minimum pension age for some workers to as low as 60, from around 63; Mr Bardella is less sure) and keep the party’s identity as a populist outfit that promises to defend ordinary working families. She is a deputy for a constituency in the mining basin of northern France.
paragraphs.zh: 对勒庞领导的“国民联盟”（RN）来说，这一决定带来了明确方向，也使该党能够开始正式竞选。勒庞将有权主导党的路线，在存在政策分歧的议题上作出决定——她希望将某些劳动者的法定最低退休年龄从约63岁降到最低60岁；而巴德拉对此并不那么确定。她也将保持该党作为一个“民粹”政党的身份，继续宣称要维护普通工薪家庭。她是法国北部矿区一个选区的国会议员。

paragraphs.en: Ms Le Pen’s candidacy, however, will not resolve all tensions within the party. Mr Bardella, who had for months geared himself up to stand for the presidency in her stead, is now relegated back into second place. The young RN president has been pushing a different electoral strategy—based on marrying the party’s traditional working-class base and its politics of indignation to a middle-class electorate—by sounding more fiscally responsible and business friendly. The pair insist that they will work together seamlessly. Indeed, the day after the verdict they made a joint trip to a market in rural France. Ms Le Pen described them as a “ticket”, and confirmed that she would make him her prime minister if she wins the presidency. But over the coming months such differences will inevitably strain the tie.
paragraphs.zh: 然而，勒庞的参选并不会消除党内所有紧张关系。巴德拉此前数月都在为取而代之参选总统做准备，现在则被重新放回第二位。这位年轻的RN党主席近来推进的是一种不同的竞选策略：通过更强调财税责任和对企业友好的一面，将该党传统的工人阶层基础和“愤怒政治”同中产选民相结合。两人都坚持称将无缝协作。事实上，在判决次日，他们共同前往法国乡村的一个集市。勒庞把他们称作“搭档”，并确认如果她赢得总统大选，将任命巴德拉担任总理。但未来几个月，这些分歧势必会不断加大两人的拉锯。

paragraphs.en: Ms Le Pen now begins her campaign with a handsome lead: she is on 36% in first-round voting, according to an Ifop poll conducted after the verdict, well ahead of either 19% for Edouard Philippe or 15% for Gabriel Attal (two of President Emmanuel Macron’s former prime ministers), depending on which of them is tested. In the run-off, polls suggest she has a chance of winning and doing just as well as Mr Bardella would have. Ms Le Pen remains a fearsome campaigner, with three presidential bids and bitter lessons learned behind her. One of France’s most popular figures, the RN leader will draw support from those who think that it is—at last—her time. “She will be very hard to beat,” says a centrist figure.
paragraphs.zh: 勒庞现在以明显优势开启竞选：根据伊福普（Ifop）在判决后进行的民意调查，她在第一轮选举中获得36%，远高于爱德华·菲利普的19%或加布里埃尔·阿塔尔的15%（两人都是现任总统埃马纽埃尔·马克龙的前总理，具体取决于哪一位入选测试对象）。在决选中，民调显示她有机会赢得选举，并且会取得与巴德拉原本可能达到的成绩相当的结果。勒庞是一位经验老到且不容轻视的竞选者，已有三次总统竞选经历，吸取了沉重教训。法国最有人气的政治人物之一，这位RN领袖将得到那些认为“终于轮到她”的选民支持。一位中间派人士表示：“她将非常难以被击败。”

paragraphs.en: Even so, polls nine months ahead of voting are seldom an accurate indicator of the final result. It remains unclear who she would be up against in the broad centre; at the moment both Mr Philippe and Mr Attal have formally declared their candidacies and are out campaigning. Ms Le Pen lacks the freshness and sense of renewal that a Bardella candidacy would have brought the party. Above all, the prospect of a leading presidential contender, however popular, possibly campaigning under house arrest with an electronic ankle tag puts France in uncharted territory.
paragraphs.zh: 即便如此，距离投票还有九个月，民意调查通常也难以准确预判最终结果。她在较广泛中间选区将面对谁仍不清晰；目前，菲利普和阿塔尔都已正式宣布参选并开始投身竞选。勒庞也缺少巴德拉参选可能带来的新鲜感和“更换感”。更重要的是，一位热门的总统主要竞争者在“可能佩戴电子脚镣、被软禁式居家监禁”下参选的前景，已将法国推向前所未有的局面。

paragraphs.en: The opposition has lost no time condemning Ms Le Pen’s decision to stand, and reminding voters that she has now been found guilty by two courts, even if the second conviction is suspended by her latest appeal. Out on the streets the day after the verdict, with Mr Bardella at her side, a defiant Ms Le Pen declared that voters “know full well” that the accusations against her “do not deserve a criminal conviction”. Mr Attal accused her of “Trumpian rhetoric”. Playing the victim may go down well with her base, which has long suspected that the judges are out to get her. But it could make it more difficult for her to widen her appeal. ■
paragraphs.zh: 反对派并未错过任何机会谴责勒庞女士参选的决定，并提醒选民，她已经被两家法院认定有罪，即使她最近上诉后，第二项定罪被暂缓执行。在宣判次日，她在街头接受采访时，巴德拉先生陪同在旁，勒庞女士强硬表示，选民“完全清楚”针对她的指控“并不值得被判刑”。阿塔尔先生指责她使用了“特朗普式修辞”。将自己塑造成受害者可能会在她的支持基础层中引起共鸣，因为他们早已怀疑法官在针对她。但这也可能使她更难扩大自身的支持面。■

paragraphs.zh: 如想及时获取最新的欧洲大事，欢迎订阅《欧洲咖啡馆》——我们的每周付费订阅通讯。
